analysis:
- Issue identified: The game is failing to load the `p5.collide2d` library as an ES module from `https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/p5.collide2d@1.0.0/+esm`, resulting in a `net::ERR_ABORTED 404 (Not Found)` error. This import in `physics.js` conflicts with the existing global loading of an older version (`0.7.3`) of `p5.collide2d` in `index.html`. Furthermore, `physics.js` incorrectly assumes `collideRectRect` is a globally available function, whereas `p5.collide2d` (when `p5.js` is already loaded) attaches its collision functions to `p5.prototype`.
- Files affected: `physics.js`
- Changes needed: Remove the problematic ES module import from `physics.js`. Instead, access the globally available `p5` object (which `p5.collide2d` extends) via `window.p5` within `physics.js`, and use `p5.prototype.collideRectRect` for collision checks. This aligns `physics.js` with the project's existing architecture of loading p5.js extensions globally.
